docs.title: normalMagic Home
docs.text:
Site under construction

Welcome to the normalMagic documentation. Here you'll find everything there is to know about the tools.

  • Install normalMagic
  • Examples

normalMagic is a versatile Blender toolset designed to give you powerful control over mesh normals and enhance modeling workflows.

Controlling normals can be essential for creating high quality 3D art. Whether it's for hard surfaces, foliage, cel shading, hair/fur... The list goes on! Modifying normals is sometimes the only way to get surfaces behaving the way you want.

Word of caution

Most of these tools create or edit custom normals.

Once custom normals exist on a mesh, some default Blender tools/modifiers might not behave as expected. Tools are included to fix situations such as mirroring or beveling but it's something to be aware of.

docs.title: Perfect boolean normals
docs.text:
• Completely avoid boolean normal issues with Boolean Pro.
  • Repair normals on previously cut geometry with Repair Boolean Normals.

docs.title: Seamlessly blend surfaces
docs.text:
• Project positions, normals and UVs with Surface Blend.
  • Merge, cut and weld projected meshes into a surface with Surface Insert.

Convenient auto-masking options means no need for creating and managing vertex groups. Sharp edges can be preserved and transferred.

docs.title: Smooth Normals
docs.text: Smooth normals to improve shading, achieve good toon lighting or for cheap subsurface scattering effects.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text: If anything is unclear or missing, please reach out! spaghetmenot@gmail.com @spaghetmenot.bsky.social @SpaghetMeNot@mastodon.social

docs.title: Tools Overview
docs.text:
normalMagic is comprised of several tools. Some modify normals, some modify geometry and normals, and some visualize normals and related attributes.

Word of caution

Most of these tools create or edit custom normals. These can be unintuitive to work with if you're unfamiliar Once custom normals exist on a mesh, some default Blender tools/modifiers might not behave as expected. Tools are included to fix situations such as mirroring or beveling but it's something to be aware of.

docs.title: Mesh Modifiers
docs.text:
These modifiers effect both normals and underlying geometry and other attributes such as UVs.

  • Boolean Pro. Replacement for Blender's built-in boolean modifier with
  • Surface Blend
  • Surface Insert

docs.title: Repair Bevel Normals
docs.text:
Beveling a mesh with custom normals can lead to unexpected behavior. This tool identifies bevels and replicates Blender's "Harden Normals" functionality found on the bevel modifier.

  • Bevel with custom normals
  • Repaired Normals

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Mesh object with beveled faces you wish to fix

  • (Recommended) Set Face Strength to "Affected" or "All" on the bevel modifier

docs.title: Bevel Detection
docs.text:
How to detect bevelled faces.

  • Face Strength. Recommended option. Requires setting face strength on bevel modifier to "Affected" or "All"

    • Sharp Bevel Edge. Apply sharp edges to bevel's outer edges.
    • Sharp Bevel Corners. Apply sharp edges to bevel's corners.

  • Face Area. Select bevels by their face area.

    • Max Bevel Area. Faces with area below this value will be treated as bevels

  • Attribute. Use an attribute or vertex group to select beveled faces.

docs.title: Area Weighting
docs.text:
For weighting normals inside the bevel. Available when Bevel Detection is set to Face Area or Attribute

  • Area Bias. Bend normals towards faces with larger areas. 1 = Direct weight based on area. Higher values will weight more aggressively to larger areas.
  • Area Cutoff. Ignores normals from smaller faces. Cutoff is a percentage of face area compared to the largest connected face

docs.title: Selection Panel
docs.text: See Selection page

docs.title: Repair Boolean Normals
docs.text: This tool does its best to fix bad normals on geometry caused by booleans. This is intended for destructively applied booleans, for boolean modifiers please consider using Boolean Pro as it provides much more accurate and consistent results

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
1. Mesh object with destructive booleans applied.
  2. Mesh is un-triangulated i.e. boolean operations have created ngons.
  3. Mesh has sharp edges on boolean seams.

Even if a mesh meets all these requirements it isn't 100% guaranteed to fix all it's normals

docs.title: Error Correct Distance
docs.text:
Welds vertices on the proxy mesh that normals are transferred from.

This can fix normal issues but will also introduce more issues as the value increases. There should be a sweet spot where there aren't any issues

docs.title: Selection Panel
docs.text: See Selection page

docs.title: How it Works
docs.text:
1. Splitting the mesh on sharp edges, producing a surface for each "normal island"
  2. Detecting points on the boundary that don't join to any edges inside the island
  3. Re-creating a version of each island without those points
  4. Transferring normals per newly created island back up to the unaltered geometry

docs.title: Repair Mirrored Normals
docs.text:
Mirroring a mesh with custom normals will not mirror the normals. This leads to incorrect normals on the mirrored geometry.

This modifier will detect mesh islands with flipped normals and what axis they need flipping on.

  • Unmirrored Custom Normals
  • Mirrored Custom Normals

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• Normal Domain. Whether normals are stored on points (smooth) or face corners (allows sharp edges)
  • Method. Choose which faces get mirrored normals:
    • Detect. Faces and axes will be determined automatically, this should work in most cases.
    • Specify. Specify which faces to flip normals and which axes to flip them on.

docs.title: Auto-Flip Faces
docs.text:
Flipped faces on meshes can cause multiple issues. Traditionally you would check for flipped faces by using the the 'Face Orientation' overlay and resolve areas of flipped faces one by one.

This modifier will detect flipped faces and fix them automatically.

  • Faces Flipped
  • Faces Un-flipped

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• Invert. Change direction of entire mesh from outside to inside
  • Hit Rate. Tune the detection of flipped faces. Higher values will detect more but increase the risk of false positives.
